findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "find_products_for_problem" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Find products built to solve a real-world problem. Describe the problem in plain language (not keywords) — e.g. 'my dad keeps missing doses of his medication'. …

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "find_products_for_problem" is 2821 chars, far longer than this server's median (284). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

confidence.title: Description uses pressure/override language
confidence.detail: Description of "find_products_for_problem" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

confidence.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
confidence.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: find_products_for_problem, get_product · Public sink: find_products_for_problem.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: find_products_for_problem, get_product · Public sink: find_products_for_problem

confidence.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
